front: What is the P class in computational complexity theory?
back: P is the class of all decision problems that can be solved by a deterministic Turing machine in polynomial time.

front: What is the NP class in computational complexity theory?
back: NP is the class of all decision problems for which a proposed solution (or 'certificate') can be verified by a deterministic Turing machine in polynomial time.

front: What is the P vs NP problem?
back: The P vs NP problem is the fundamental unsolved question in computer science asking whether every problem that can be verified quickly (in NP) can also be solved quickly (in P). It asks if P = NP.

front: What does it mean for a problem to be NP-complete?
back: A problem is NP-complete if it is in NP and every other problem in NP can be reduced to it in polynomial time. If any NP-complete problem has a polynomial-time algorithm, then P = NP.

The Cook-Levin Theorem states that the Boolean Satisfiability Problem (SAT) is NP-complete. It was the first problem proven to be NP-complete, providing a foundation for the theory of NP-completeness.
